commit.message:
fix(e2e): avoid Keycloak redirect DNS in stack smoke health checks

Playwright followed 302 to http://keycloak:8080; that hostname is not resolvable on the CI host. Use maxRedirects: 0 and treat 302 to OAuth like the curl-based wait loop.

Co-authored-by: GregorGullwi <1397711+GregorGullwi@users.noreply.github.com>

* Finish deferred alias materialization slice

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/GregorGullwi/FlashCpp/sessions/2475041f-2f4d-4b85-ae61-1ce02fd8aaef

Co-authored-by: GregorGullwi <1397711+GregorGullwi@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix pre-existing bug: restore consumed '::' token when not followed by identifier

In finalizeInstantiatedAliasType, when peek() == '::', the token was
consumed via advance() but if the next token was not an identifier the
code fell through silently, dropping the '::'. Use save/restore token
position to roll back when the speculative parse fails.

* Simplify reference check to single enum comparison

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: Enable testTagsAsResourceId for Maestro id: selectors (#308)

Adds testTagsAsResourceId = true to Scaffold's semantics modifier
to expose Compose testTags as resource IDs in the accessibility tree.
This allows Maestro flows to find elements using id: selectors
(e.g., id: 'nav_history', id: 'search_bar').

Without this, Maestro's id: selector cannot find testTag elements
because Compose does not expose testTags as resource-id by default.

Fixes #307

Co-authored-by: Copilot <copilot@github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
BugFix: Skip verify_cifs_basic on FIPS-enabled images (#4412)

* [AI Generated] BugFix: Skip verify_cifs_basic on FIPS-enabled images

CIFS/SMB mounts use NTLMSSP authentication which relies on MD5-based
signing. FIPS mode disallows MD5, causing the mount to fail. Add a
check for /proc/sys/crypto/fips_enabled at the start of the test to
skip gracefully instead of failing. Use node.execute() instead of
Cat.read() to handle the case where the file does not exist on
non-FIPS images.

* Use (fips_result.stdout or "").strip() to safely handle None stdout

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/microsoft/lisa/sessions/48b84421-4dc8-413c-9bd7-90628b7cdc98

Co-authored-by: LiliDeng <10083705+LiliDeng@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Johnson George <johgeorg@microsoft.com>
Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix /create-issue workflow (#15988)

* Isolate CreateFailingTestIssue tool from Arcade SDK

Add Directory.Build.props/targets/Packages.props to cut MSBuild
property inheritance from the repo root. Switch Aspire.TestTools from
ProjectReference to Compile Include so the tool has no transitive
dependency on Arcade.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Support positional test names in /create-issue

`/create-issue TestName` now works without requiring --test.
Ambiguous inputs (mixing positional + --test, or multiple bare args)
are rejected with a clear error in both orderings.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update github-script to v8 and replace restore.sh with setup-dotnet

Bump actions/github-script from v7.0.1 to v8 (Node 22) across all
workflow steps. Replace the full restore.sh SDK install with
actions/setup-dotnet using global.json, since the isolated tool
only needs the .NET SDK — not the full repo restore.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Extract formatListResponse helper with error handling

Move inline list-mode logic into a testable function. Returns a
structured result with error/message/tests fields, adding proper
error reporting when the resolver step fails without producing output.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add tests for positional parsing and formatListResponse

Cover positional test name with flags, ambiguity in both orderings,
and formatListResponse for resolver failure, success with tests, and
empty results.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Surface resolver errors when result has no tests

When the C# tool exits non-zero but writes JSON (success: false), and
no test names are present, surface the errorMessage instead of showing
a misleading 'No test failures were found' message.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Let formatListResponse own the message format for all paths

Move the bulleted backtick-wrapped formatting into the helper so the
workflow caller just uses listResult.message directly. Also add the
missing # v4 version comment on setup-dotnet.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

commit.message:
fix(autoupdate): set UpdateStatus based on NupkgPath, add package filter for workflow_dispatch

Previously, UpdateStatus was set to 'Update Available' when AU detected a newer remote version,
even if no .nupkg was built (e.g. version already exists in Chocolatey community feed).
This caused 'dotnet nuget push' to fail with a null path error.

Fix: derive UpdateStatus from NupkgPath so push steps only run when a package was actually built.

Also adds a package input to workflow_dispatch so individual packages can be tested manually.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix HAP SRP pairing: downgrade to srp 0.5 + working pair_setup.rs

Replace dependency-updates branch pair_setup.rs with the working main
branch version, using srp 0.5 APIs (SrpClient, UserRecord, get_key).
Downgrade sha2 0.10→0.9, hkdf 0.12→0.11, chacha20poly1305 0.9→0.8 to
match srp 0.5's digest 0.9 requirement. Add rand 0.7. Keep libmdns 0.7
to preserve the ifaddrs linker fix.

Resolves 'setup code is incorrect' errors. Full M1→M6 handshake now
completes and iOS successfully pairs with the bridge.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: race condition in spectrum double-buffer flip

- Make accum_idx volatile (shared cross-core state)
- Reset accum_idx BEFORE flipping write_buf_ so Core 1 never
  reads a buffer that Core 0 is still indexing into
- sample_ready set last as the release signal

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(order-processor): narrow exception handler and add docstrings (H-01, L-01)

## Summary
This commit resolves two findings from architecture review:
- **H-01 (High)**: Broad exception handler in _parse_expiry() masked programming errors
- **L-01 (Low)**: Missing docstrings reduced code maintainability

## Changes
1. **Narrowed exception handler** (_parse_expiry):
   - Changed: except Exception as exc: → except (ValueError, IndexError) as exc:
   - Reason: Only ValueError and IndexError can occur from split() and date().
   - Benefit: Unexpected errors now propagate, improving debuggability.

2. **Added PEP 257 docstrings** to three functions:
   - _to_decimal(): Explains Decimal conversion for monetary values
   - _parse_expiry(): Explains date parsing with expected format
   - _validate_item(): Explains item validation constraints
   - Standard format: one-line summary, Args, Returns, Raises sections

## Quality Gate Results
- ruff: ✅ 0 violations
- mypy: ✅ 0 type errors
- pytest: ✅ 25/25 tests passed (100% success rate)

## Pipeline Artifacts
- architecture-findings.md: ✅ APPROVED
- planning-fix.md: ✅ APPROVED
- test-report.md: ✅ ALL GATES PASSED

No breaking changes. All existing tests pass. Code is backward compatible.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(build): prevent 0-byte static pages from I/O contention

- Reduce parallel write batch from 300 to 200 to lower I/O pressure
- Add sync repair pass after flush: detects and rewrites 0-byte files
- Log individual write failures with file paths (was only logging count)
- flatContentPlugin: skip copying 0-byte source files to flat paths

Root cause: writeFile uses O_TRUNC which creates 0-byte files before
writing content. Under heavy I/O contention (153K+ parallel writes),
some writes fail after truncation, leaving 0-byte files that Google
indexes as soft-404s.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add native-language altnames to existing bridge schemas

Add lang:XX altnames to JsonStructure schema properties where upstream
API field names were translated from a non-English language to English.

Bridges updated:
- imgw-hydro: Polish (lang:pl) altnames for 12 fields across Station
  and WaterLevelObservation schemas
- bfs-odl: German (lang:de) altnames added alongside existing json
  altnames for station_id, station_code, postal_code
- rws-waterwebservices: Dutch (lang:nl) altnames for name,
  coordinate_system, location_name, timestamp, value, quality_code,
  status
- syke-hydro: Finnish (lang:fi) altnames for station_id, name,
  river_name, water_area_name, municipality, water_level,
  water_level_timestamp, discharge, discharge_timestamp
- german-waters: German (lang:de) altnames for water_body, altitude
- dwd: German (lang:de) altnames for STATIONS_ID, MESS_DATUM, QN,
  and DWD measurement codes (PP_10, TT_10, etc.) across all CDC
  schemas

hubeau-hydrometrie skipped: schema already uses native French field
names (code_station, libelle_station, etc.) with no translation.

Avro schemas are unchanged.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: move FFT to Core 1 to eliminate audio jitter

Core 0 callback now only accumulates mono samples into a double buffer
(~microseconds). FFT computation runs on Core 1 in draw_frame_() where
the ~100μs cost is negligible within the 55-78ms frame time.

- Double-buffered sample capture: Core 0 writes, flips index when full
- spectrum_compute() called from draw_frame_ before reading bands
- Switched to magnitude² (skip sqrtf), adjusted log10 scaling
- Zero FFT overhead on audio core — eliminates streaming jitter

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add Spanish altnames to Madrid Traffic xreg schema

commit.message:
fix: add 5s grace period for duplicated tabs

Newly created/duplicated tabs get a 5-second grace period before
auto-close kicks in, giving the user time to navigate to a different URL.
Manual 'Close Duplicates' button is unaffected.
